questions.text: What is the primary engineering goal for this deployment?
questions.description: Choose the metric you care about optimizing above all else.

options.label: Maximum Accuracy
options.description: I need the absolute highest classification correctness on complex images, regardless of model size or runtime cost.

options.label: Real-time Inference Speed
options.description: I need low-latency, high-throughput execution (e.g. live video streams, high frame rates).

options.label: Low VRAM / Memory Constraints
options.description: The target system has limited RAM (e.g., edge devices, standard laptops, mobile app bundles).

options.label: Research & Educational Study
options.description: I want to study the core concepts of convolutional design, parameter mapping, or historical breakthroughs.

questions.text: What is your target hardware deployment environment?
questions.description: This determines the physical device where inference will execute.

options.label: Cloud Server / High-End GPU
options.description: NVIDIA GPUs, TPUs, or server nodes with rich memory resources.

options.label: Smartphone (iOS / Android)
options.description: Mobile app edge runtime using CoreML, TensorFlow Lite, or PyTorch Mobile.

options.label: Embedded Board / IoT / Microcontroller
options.description: Raspberry Pi, Jetson Nano, Arduino, or TinyML hardware with strict power boundaries.

questions.text: What is the available computational complexity budget?
questions.description: This maps directly to FLOPs (Floating Point Operations) allowed during forward passes.

options.label: Strictly limited budget
options.description: Minimize operations to save power, heat, and battery life.

options.label: Balanced computational budget
options.description: A reasonable trade-off between throughput and accuracy.

options.label: High performance / Unlimited budget
options.description: Willing to spend massive computational FLOPs to gain incremental accuracy.
